description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar panel array, supported by a single central pole anchored into a concrete base. The array consists of twelve blue photovoltaic panels arranged in a 3x4 grid, each panel featuring a grid of white electrical contacts. The panels are framed in black metal and are tilted at an angle to optimize sunlight capture. The supporting structure includes a robust black metal frame with circular cutouts for weight reduction and stability. The concrete base provides a solid foundation, ensuring the array remains upright and secure. No visible electrical cabling or inverter components are present on the exterior.
description: A pole-mounted solar panel array with twelve blue photovoltaic panels in a 3x4 grid, supported by a black metal frame and concrete base.
description_view_0: Side view showing the tilted blue solar panels, black metal frame, and central pole anchored in concrete.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the black frame structure, central support pole, and the flat alignment of the panel array.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the angled solar panels and support structure.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the full 3x4 grid of blue solar panels and the central support pole.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed but may be adjustable
attribute_mounting_structure_type: pole-mounted, supported by a steel or aluminum frame anchored in a concrete base
attribute_array_configuration: 3x4 grid of solar panels arranged in a single rectangular array
